title: OT Sector to NACSA Code of Practice Cross-Reference
description: Maps OT sectors to relevant NACSA Act 854 codes of practice (indicative instruments). For each sector, identifies the OT-specific requirements that the code of practice adds on top of base Act 854 obligations and IEC 62443 baseline.
_mappingNote: Maps OT sectors to NACSA codes of practice documents, not to individual control slugs. References are to sector-specific code of practice files, not nacsa controls/library.json entries.
verificationNote: NACSA codes of practice under Act 854 were not gazetted as of 2026-03. Mappings reference indicative codes from the nacsa repo (github.com/dawuds/nacsa). Verify against gazetted instruments when published.

otSpecificOverlays: IEC 61850 implementation requirements for substation automation
NERC CIP-equivalent controls for grid-connected assets
Emergency Power System (EPS) cybersecurity requirements
Real-time OT monitoring requirements for transmission assets
Energy Management System (EMS) access control requirements
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 3 minimum for TNB transmission and generation assets; SL 2 for distribution; SL 4 for SIS at generation plants
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Energy Commission Act 2001 (Act 610); Electricity Supply Act 1990 (Act 447)

otSpecificOverlays: Chemical dosing system input validation and hardware interlock requirements
Water quality monitoring system integrity requirements
OT monitoring for detection of process manipulation
Emergency manual override capability for all automated water treatment functions
Incident response procedures specific to public health emergency scenarios
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 3-4 for chemical dosing control; SL 2-3 for pumping and distribution; SL 2 for monitoring systems
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Water Services Industry Act 2006 (Act 655); National Water Services Commission Act 2006 (Act 654)
sectorMappings.publicHealthNote: Any compromise of chemical dosing controls is a public health emergency. SL 3-4 for dosing zones is non-negotiable regardless of NCII designation status.

sectorMappings.nacsaCop: No specific NACSA cop — covered under energy/critical infrastructure framework
otSpecificOverlays: SIS/ESD/F&G security requirements aligned with IEC 61511
Process safety management (PSM) integration with OT security programme
Pipeline SCADA security requirements
Offshore platform OT security requirements (limited physical access)
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 4 for SIS/ESD/F&G; SL 3 for DCS and pipeline SCADA; SL 2 for monitoring
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Petroleum Development Act 1974; PCSB (PETRONAS Carigali) operational requirements; DOSH safety regulations
sectorMappings.tritонNote: TRITON/TRISIS attack (2017) targeted petrochemical SIS. All Petronas and downstream oil/gas operators running Triconex, Honeywell SM, or Yokogawa ProSafe-RS must implement SIS-specific security controls from this repository.

otSpecificOverlays: Rail signalling system security requirements (ETCS/CBTC, ATP/ATO)
Aviation safety system security (ILS, ATIS, ATC integration)
Port vessel traffic service (VTS) security requirements
Traffic management centre (TMC) security requirements
Separation of safety-critical from operational OT systems
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 3-4 for rail signalling; SL 3-4 for aviation safety systems; SL 3 for VTS; SL 2-3 for road TMC
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Railways Act 1991 (Act 463); Civil Aviation Act 1969 (Act 3); Merchant Shipping Ordinance 1952

sectorMappings.nacsaCop: No specific NACSA cop for manufacturing
otSpecificOverlays: Industry 4.0 / IIoT security requirements
Remote monitoring and diagnostic security for smart manufacturing
Production data and intellectual property protection requirements
Cloud-connected OT security requirements
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 2-3 for process control; SL 2 for production monitoring; SL 3 for safety-critical manufacturing (pharma, chemical, semiconductor)
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Factories and Machinery Act 1967 (Act 139); DOSH requirements

sectorMappings.nacsaCop: No specific NACSA cop — government buildings under Government sector
sectorMappings.sectorLead: JKR for government buildings; MAMPU for digital infrastructure
otSpecificOverlays: Data centre BMS security requirements (availability-critical)
Hospital HVAC/clean room security requirements (patient safety)
Integration security for converged BMS/IT platforms
Physical access control system security requirements
sectorMappings.iec62443SLRecommendation: SL 2-3 for data centre BMS; SL 2 for hospital environmental controls; SL 1-2 for commercial BMS
sectorMappings.regulatoryOverlap: Uniform Building By-Laws 1984; Fire Services Act 1988 (Act 341)
